message.content: The ocean appears blue primarily due to the way water absorbs and scatters sunlight. Sunlight contains a spectrum of colors, with blue light having a shorter wavelength and being scattered more than other colors as it passes through water. While water absorbs colors like red and yellow more efficiently, blue wavelengths are reflected back, creating the characteristic blue hue of ocean waters. Additionally, the clarity and depth of the water, as well as the presence of particles and microorganisms, can also influence the specific shade of blue that we
